2605.10059.title: Strategic Exploitation in LLM Agent Markets: A Simulation Framework for E-Commerce Trust
2605.10059.paper_summary: Agent-based modeling (ABM) has long been used in economics to study human behavior, and large language model (LLM) agents now enable new forms of social and economic simulation. While prior work has discovered strategic deception by LLM agents in financial trading and auction markets, e-commerce remains underexplored despite its distinctive information asymmetry: sellers privately observe product quality, whereas buyers rely on advertised claims and reputation signals. We introduce TruthMarketTwin, a controlled simulation framework for studying LLM-agent behavior in e-commerce markets. The framework is one of the first to model bilateral trade under asymmetric information sharing, where agents make strategic listing, purchasing, rating, and recourse-related decisions to optimize seller profit and buyer utility. We find that LLM agents released into traditional markets autonomously exploit weaknesses in reputation-based governance, while warrant enforcement reduces deception and reshapes strategic reasoning. Our results position LLM-agent simulation as a tool for studying institution-governed autonomous markets.
2605.10059.paper_summary_zh: 代理人基礎模型（ABM）長期以來在經濟學中被用來研究人類行為，而大型語言模型（LLM）代理人現在使社會和經濟模擬出現新的形式。<lb/>雖然先前的研究已經發現LLM代理人在金融交易和拍賣市場中的策略性欺騙，但電子商務仍然未被充分探索，儘管其獨特的信息不對稱：賣方私下觀察產品質量，而買方則依賴廣告聲明和聲譽信號。<lb/>我們介紹了TruthMarketTwin，一個用於研究LLM代理人在電子商務市場中行為的受控模擬框架。<lb/>該框架是首批在不對稱信息共享下對雙邊貿易進行建模的框架之一，其中代理人做出戰略性的列表、購買、評級和追索相關決策，以優化賣方利潤和買方效用。<lb/>我們發現，釋放到傳統市場中的LLM代理人自主利用基於聲譽的治理中的弱點，而保證執行則減少了欺騙並重塑了戰略推理。<lb/>我們的結果將LLM代理人模擬定位為研究制度治理的自主市場的工具。
2605.10059.author: Shijun Lei et.al.
2605.10059.authors: Shijun Lei, Quang Nguyen, Swapneel S Mehta, Zeping Li, Huichuan Fu, Xiaolong Zheng, Siki Chen, Yunji Liang, Philip Torr, Zhenfei Yin
